title: Runtime / Abuse / Incident Hardening - Production IAM Baseline
decision_rationale: GL-208 documents and tests the runtime, IAM, abuse, incident, and security-event baseline without broad rewrites. Existing controls already provide production-like fail-closed config checks, admin/operator bearer-token separation, server-derived tenant context, safe admin operator responses, deterministic in-memory rate limiting, correlation IDs, and secret-safe structured logging for Developer Preview / Controlled Preview. These controls are not complete production IAM, production-grade DDoS protection, a complete incident program, or a complete production observability stack. Production SaaS and real/private data remain no-go.

current_state_summary.posture: Developer Preview / Controlled Preview with strict boundaries
existing_controls: Operator bearer-token boundary with PBKDF2 and lookup hash
Safe admin operator list/read response fields
Structured logging with correlation IDs and redaction

runtime_mode_assessment.test: deterministic tests and local tokens allowed
runtime_mode_assessment.demo: requires admin token by default; not production
runtime_mode_assessment.controlled_preview: bounded operational posture using synthetic/demo data only; not production SaaS

runtime_hardening_summary.implemented: No new backend runtime code was needed; existing GL-201 fail-closed checks are verified by GL-208 tests.
fail_closed_controls: placeholder/short admin token rejected in production-like modes
placeholder/short bootstrap operator token rejected in production-like modes
runtime_hardening_summary.protected_routes: Resource, demo-action, demo tamper, and admin/operator routes remain protected.
runtime_hardening_summary.cors: Exact-match allowlist only; no wildcard and no arbitrary origin reflection.

production_iam_baseline_assessment.admin: Bearer token with constant-time comparison; fail-closed when required.
production_iam_baseline_assessment.operator: PBKDF2-SHA256 token hash, SHA-256 lookup hash, active-row filtering, expiry handling.

admin_operator_iam_boundary.one_time_create_token: Raw operator token may be returned only on create; it is not persisted or returned in list/read/revoke responses.

tenant_workspace_preservation_assessment.tenant_context: server-derived from operator row or admin demo binding

severity_classes.P0: credential exposure, audit/evidence integrity compromise, startup bypass, or controlled-preview real/private data use
severity_classes.P1: repeated auth failures, rate-limit events, migration failure, or backup/restore drill failure
severity_classes.P2: documentation drift, stale claim, or non-sensitive controlled-preview process issue

observability_security_event_baseline.migration_backup_restore_signals: Documented in GL-205; no external backend added.

implementation_summary.rate_limit_helper_reason: Existing backend/src/rate_limiter.py is deterministic and already wired into server route groups; GL-208 tests preserve it.

controlled_preview_impact.expansion: no expansion to real customer/private grant/institutional data

production_readiness_impact.abuse_protection: baseline only, not production-grade DDoS protection
production_readiness_impact.incident_response: baseline only, not complete production incident program
production_readiness_impact.observability: baseline only, not complete production observability stack

remaining_blockers: Production IAM with OAuth/JWT/SSO and rotation workflows
Production observability, alerting, tracing, and retention
Production-grade abuse controls and edge protection

risk_register.risk: Admin token exposure grants broad control-plane access
risk_register.mitigation: Keep tokens out of logs/responses; rotate by environment change; replace with production IAM later

risk_register.risk: In-memory rate limiter is not sufficient for production abuse
risk_register.mitigation: Document as baseline only; add edge and distributed controls later
Admin can revoke and recreate; production tenant admin workflow remains deferred

risk_register.risk: Incident baseline is too lightweight for production
risk_register.mitigation: Treat as controlled-preview baseline and create production incident program later

risk_register.risk: No external observability backend means no production alerting
risk_register.mitigation: Keep production SaaS no-go until observability stack exists

findings: Existing rate limiter/helper is deterministic and tested.
Operator tokens cannot access admin-only routes.
Arbitrary tenant override headers remain unsupported.
Raw token/hash leakage is prevented in safe admin list/read responses.
No OpenAPI, migration, frontend, workflow, SDK package, or website/design change was required.
recommended_next_issues: GL-206B Live PostgreSQL Validation Execution when ephemeral PostgreSQL is available
